Note:
(7) VONB of each segment includes retirement business, presented on a local statutory basis, before unallocated Group Office expenses and before non-controlling interests.
(1) From 2017 onwards, financial information is presented on a financial year ended 31 December basis. Financial information for 2016 is presented on a financial year ended 30 November basis.

(8) From 2019 onwards, ANP and VONB for other markets include our share of results from our 49% equity interest in Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Limited (Tata AIA Life). ANP and VONB reported in 2018 or earlier have not been restated and do not include any contribution from Tata AIA Life. From 2019 onwards, the Group's VONB excludes VONB attributable to non-controlling interests. VONB for 2018 or earlier has not been restated and, as previously disclosed, was not adjusted for non-controlling interests. Tata AIA Life's International Financial Reporting Standards results are calculated using the equity method. The Group's consolidated financial statements use Tata AIA Life financial data on a one-quarter lag basis. For clarity, total weighted premium income does not include any contribution from Tata AIA Life.

(2) VONB is the present value of the estimated post-tax statutory profits expected to be generated in the future from new business sold during the period, calculated at the time of sale, less the cost of funds required to be held in excess of statutory reserves to support the new business.
(3) ANP is a measure of the level of new business, calculated as the sum of 100% of annualized first-year premiums and 10% of single premiums, before reinsurance ceded.
(4) Operating Profit After Tax presented excludes non-controlling interests.
(5) Total Weighted Premium Income includes renewal premiums of 100% before reinsurance ceded, first year premiums of 100%, and single premiums of 10%.

(9) AIA has changed its accounting treatment under International Financial Reporting Standards for the recognition and measurement of insurance contract liabilities for its Hong Kong participating business to reflect expected changes in policyholder dividends. Comparative data for 2019 has been adjusted. Comparative data for 2018 and prior years have not been restated.

(6) EV is determined using actuarial methods based on a series of specific assumptions about future experience to estimate the economic value of life insurance business, but excludes the economic value of any future new business. EV Equity is the sum of EV, goodwill and other intangible assets.
2020 Annual Report 007
Overview
Overview
Chairman's Statement
Over the past century, AIA
In Asia, for those who greatly need our services
renowned for protecting communities,
We take great pride in this.
Mr. Tse Sze Wing Independent Non-Executive Chairman
During the COVID-19 pandemic,
Our colleagues care for the community and fulfill their duties,
Serving millions of customers
Providing continuous support.
AIA has a long and storied history in Asia. Through various periods of social upheaval and uncertainty, we have managed our business
prudently and earned a distinguished reputation, demonstrating that AIA is trustworthy, resilient and steadfast in doing the right thing for
our stakeholders. Since early 2020, the COVID-19 pandemic has created a constantly evolving and increasingly complex business
environment, with unprecedented shifts in the political, macroeconomic and capital markets landscape. In such circumstances, AIA's

financial strength and our purpose of helping people live healthier, longer, better lives take on even greater significance. I am deeply grateful to all AIA employees, agents and partners who, despite facing the most severe challenges, have continued to care for our communities, fulfilled their responsibilities with dedication, and remained focused on serving our customers,
support to their families and our communities.
COVID-19 control measures impacted new business sales, with VONB declining to US$2.765 billion. We adopted innovative digital
tools to enhance operational efficiency, and with relaxed movement restrictions, sales momentum recovered strongly and continued into early 2021.
VONB grew 15% in the first two months of 2021 versus the same period last year.
OPAT increased by 5% to US$5.942 billion, with UFSG rising by 7% to US$5.843 billion, reflecting our high-quality recurring earnings
base. EV Equity reached a record high of US$67.2 billion. As at 31 December 2020, the Group LCSM cover ratio stood at 374%,
demonstrating our strong capital position and prudent financial management.
The Board recommends a final dividend of 100.30 Hong Kong cents per share, up 7.5%, reflecting AIA's robust and resilient financial

performance and the Board's confidence in the Group's future prospects. This brings the 2020 total dividend to 135.30 Hong Kong cents per share. The Board adheres to AIA's long-established prudent and sustainable
and progressive dividend policy, enabling the Group to seize future growth opportunities while maintaining financial flexibility.
008 AIA Group Limited
In the fourth quarter of 2020, we celebrated AIA's tenth anniversary of listing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. AIA's listing set
multiple records and remains the largest initial public offering in the global insurance industry. I feel privileged to work alongside the
Board members. AIA's business has continued to expand, becoming the world's largest life insurance company by market capitalisation.
All non-executive Directors are independent individuals with extensive governance and leadership experience in both public and private

sectors. We are committed to maintaining a corporate culture that adheres to the highest international corporate governance standards. We firmly believe that a deeply rooted corporate governance culture, combined with sound risk
Governance structure is the foundation for sustainable development.
One of the Board's key responsibilities is to ensure a strong pipeline of leadership talent and to execute effective succession planning.
We are pleased to welcome Mr. Lee Yuen Cheung as Group Chief Executive Officer and President, succeeding Mr. Wong King Fai

upon his retirement on 1 June 2020. On behalf of the Board, I would like to express our deep appreciation to Mr. Lee Yuen Cheung and his management team for their outstanding leadership during this unprecedented environment, as they continue to drive our business forward. We also wish to extend our heartfelt thanks to Mr. Wong King Fai
Years of outstanding leadership and significant contributions to AIA.
Environmental, Social and Governance issues are increasingly important globally. We are committed to driving society towards a better
future, and our ESG initiatives were recognized again in 2020. According to Sustainalytics, AIA ranked second among over 270
insurance companies, demonstrating our excellent corporate governance. Additionally, we continued to receive the "Prime" ESG
Corporate Rating from Institutional Shareholder Services Inc. (ISS), recognizing us as one of the industry's best-performing companies
for sustainable development. We believe further measures are needed to achieve better outcomes for the community. This remains a priority for me and the Board.
A key priority shared by our Board, senior management team and all employees.
Today, customer demand for AIA's products, services and high-quality advice has never been more pressing. Our strategy is built on
Asia's strong structural growth drivers supported by unprecedented wealth creation, rapidly widening protection gaps, evolving
consumer preferences, widespread digital technology adoption, and market focus on long-term sustainable development. The Board is

confident AIA is well-positioned to seize significant opportunities across the region, providing financial protection to customers and their families when needed most, while creating shared value for all stakeholders.
Finally, through the trust of our customers and shareholders, AIA has delivered strong performance and sustained growth since listing. On behalf of the Board of Directors, I
Thank you sincerely for your continued support.
Xie Shi-rong
Independent Non-Executive Chairman
March 12, 2021
Growth rates are presented at constant exchange rates to provide a clearer illustration of year-on-year business performance.
2020 Annual Report 009
Overview
Group Chief Executive and President's Report
AIA delivered exceptionally strong performance, reflecting our results
The result of our excellent team's relentless efforts. Our employees respond quickly
and adapt swiftly to the rapidly changing business environment, which fills me with pride.
With steadfast commitment, we help customers navigate uncertainty
environment while accelerating implementation of our strategic plans.
Mr. Lee Yuen Cheung Group Chief Executive Officer and President
010 AIA Group Limited
Providing uninterrupted
support.
demonstrating agility and commitment to duty.
Since the pandemic onset, our business teams responded swiftly and actively assisted in mitigating community impact while ensuring
employee safety. The Group implemented a wide range of measures including financial support, medical supplies provision,

complimentary coverage, healthcare resource access, streamlined service processes and accelerated claims payments. We identified every possible reason to make claim payments, paying out over US$16 billion in insurance
Benefits and claims provide essential financial protection.
Despite COVID-19 containment measures reducing VONB to US$2.765 billion, sales momentum turned positive as lockdown
restrictions eased, with strong momentum in early 2021, VONB growing 15% year-on-year in the first two months. Our large and
growing in-force policy portfolio generated high-quality recurring income, driving 5% OPAT growth in 2020, with UFSG growing 7%. As
at 31 December 2020, EV Equity reached a record US$67.2 billion, with Group LCSM cover ratio at 374%, which is
testify to our strong capital and prudent financial management.
The Board recommends an increase of 7.5% in the final dividend for 2020 to 100.30 Hong Kong cents per share. This reflects AIA's long-established prudent, sustainable and progressive dividend policy
policy, enabling the Group to capitalize on future growth opportunities while maintaining financial flexibility.
AIA possesses multiple distinctive competitive advantages that set us apart in a highly competitive market. With decades of operations
across Asia, we have an unparalleled platform in the region and hold leading market positions in most of the markets where we operate.

Our unwavering 100% focus on the Asian market enables us to fully capitalize on growth opportunities in the world's most attractive life insurance region. This focus, combined with our leadership team's extensive experience in Asia and our employees' exceptional capabilities,
has been key to our strong performance.
Our unparalleled distribution strength enables us to reach customers directly and provide high-quality professional advice to help meet
the substantial and growing long-term protection and savings needs of Asian consumers. Today, the public increasingly expects

companies to address their needs in ways that are consistent with long-term sustainable development. AIA possesses a market-leading brand, built on the foundation of helping people achieve their goals of living healthier, longer and better lives.
We aim to position AIA as a global leader in technology application, strengthen interconnectivity, expand scale and enhance efficiency.

Deeper data analytics insights enable us to create unique and personalized customer experiences. Products and services aligned with individual needs and lifestyles have transformed public perceptions of life insurance.
approach, laying a solid foundation for long-term customer relationships.
Despite 2020 being unprecedented, I am more optimistic than ever about the life insurance industry prospects in Asia and AIA's future.
We continue to challenge ourselves and develop strategic priorities to meet evolving customer expectations and financial needs. Our
new corporate strategy builds on AIA's existing strengths and advances our transformation to deliver superior service to customers.
2020 Annual Report 011
Overview
Group Chief Executive and President's Report
2020 Performance Highlights
AIA China delivered the largest contribution to the Group's VONB in 2020 and performed exceptionally in early 2021, with very
strong VONB growth in the first two months compared with the same period in 2020. Although 2020 VONB of US$968 million

decreased 17% from 2019, this was primarily due to limited new sales during the early COVID-19 outbreak in the first quarter. OPAT growth of 14% during the year was mainly attributable to our increasingly growing
in-force portfolio and favorable claims experience.
In 2020, we successfully obtained regulatory approval to restructure our Shanghai branch into Mainland China's first wholly
foreign-owned life insurance subsidiary. In March 2021, we received approval from the China Banking and Insurance Regulatory

Commission to commence operations in Sichuan Province. As the sixth largest province in Mainland China by GDP and the fourth largest by population, Sichuan Province presents significant long-term growth opportunities for AIA.
Hong Kong implemented travel restrictions in early February 2020, effectively halting new business sales from Mainland
Chinese visitors and causing significant VONB decline. Agency distribution achieved double-digit sales growth from local customers in
H2, maintaining significant market leadership. Partnership distribution VONB was materially lower than 2019 due to historically higher
Mainland visitor sales through retail independent financial adviser channels. OPAT grew 10%, driven by strong renewal premium growth and favorable claims experience.
AIA's Thailand business delivered very strong second-half performance. Although full-year VONB declined 4%, monthly
performance showed positive momentum with movement restrictions easing, and second-half VONB increased 33% versus the first half.
Singapore's business also demonstrated excellent improvement in the second half, with VONB increasing 56% compared to the first half and 12% year-on-year.
Full-year VONB declined 5%, as our offshore partner business was impacted by stringent border control measures, offsetting growth from the agency channel. OPAT
increased 8%.
In Malaysia, VONB declined by 13% for the full year, particularly reflecting the strict movement restrictions implemented in the first half.
VONB recovered in the second half, growing 72% compared to the first half.
In other markets, VONB in H2 increased 10% versus H1, with positive recoveries in South Korea and Indonesia. Overall,
full-year VONB declined 4%, primarily due to declines in New Zealand, the Philippines and Indonesia. Remaining markets recorded positive VONB growth, with Vietnam,
Taiwan and India recorded double-digit growth in 2020 compared to 2019.
On the agency channel front, we shifted to online recruitment and onboarding, supporting double-digit growth in new recruits and agency managers.
Although face-to-face meetings were restricted during the pandemic, resulting in a 28% decline in VONB, VONB rebounded strongly in
the second half as restrictions eased and businesses adopted new digital tools. Positive sales momentum continued into 2021, with agency business in the first two
recorded strong New Business Value growth in the first two months.
Travel restrictions on Mainland Chinese visitors to Hong Kong impacted our retail independent financial adviser business,
causing a 41% decline in VONB for our partner business in 2020. Bancassurance delivered more resilient performance, contributing the
majority of VONB for this channel. Our bancassurance partnerships with Bank Central Asia (BCA) in Indonesia, Bangkok Bank Public
Company Limited in Thailand, and Public Bank Berhad in Malaysia all recorded positive VONB growth in 2020.
012 AIA Group Limited
People First
AIA's 23,000 employees are a significant competitive advantage and key to our continued success. We promote inclusive and collaborative
learning culture and provide platforms for employees to develop meaningful careers at AIA. In response to social distancing measures
and travel restrictions, we transitioned learning content to the online "AIA Learning Hub". The AIA Leadership Centre (ALC) in Bangkok

has been redesigned to deliver our flagship leadership development programme with over 100 fully virtualised learning modules. Participants provided very positive feedback on these virtual programmes.
feedback comparable to previous in-person learning feedback.
Our annual employee engagement survey measures collective efforts across markets to better understand and support employees. In
2020, 97% of employees participated, achieving a record high engagement score at the 88th percentile against the Gallup Global

Financial Services and Insurance benchmark. AIA
was also named on Forbes' 'World's Best Employers 2020' list.
In October 2020, AIA celebrated its 10th anniversary of listing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and established the "AIA Scholarship"
programme, committing US$100 million to support 100 undergraduate students annually from various universities in Hong Kong over the coming decades, nurturing the next generation of business leaders.
Core components of the 'Organisation of the Future' strategy include strengthening and streamlining organisational structure, introducing
flexible working arrangements, and enhancing the capability framework. These strategic priorities support employee career development and strengthen AIA's position as an employer of choice.
Since the second half of 2020, we have embarked on a transformational journey through AIA's businesses in Singapore and Vietnam as
two pilot units. Initial results have delivered a more streamlined, customer-centric operating model with flexible working arrangements
and cross-functional expert teams. We plan to experiment and learn within these pilots before expanding the "Organisation of the Future" programme to other operations.
ESG
Over more than a century, AIA has made a positive and lasting impact on the lives of our customers across Asia. Our communities face
numerous environmental, social and governance issues, including climate change, environmental pollution and lifestyle-related

diseases. As the pandemic continues, it has highlighted the complexity of these challenges. As a lifelong partner to our customers and communities, we are committed to doing our part to drive society towards a better and more sustainable future.
AIA participates in the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures, a global organization establishing a voluntary and
consistent disclosure framework on climate change impacts. We continue to reaffirm our commitment to its recommendations, engaging
in 2020 with over 1,400 companies in our investment portfolio and more than 30 external investment managers to influence their management of climate change risks and climate-related issues.
According to Sustainalytics, AIA ranked second consecutively in Environmental, Social and Governance risk management in the
insurance industry, and in 2020, MSCI upgraded AIA's Environmental, Social and Governance rating to A, primarily due to our integration of Environmental, Social and Governance factors into investm
responsibilities.
2020 Annual Report 013
Overview
Group Chief Executive and President's Report
We understand that we still have a long way to go in this important area. We have also recently completed a review of our

Environmental, Social and Governance strategy. I am confident that our five strategic focus areas—enhancing physical and mental wellbeing, green operations, sustainable investment, talent and culture, and effective governance—will help us achieve our aspiration to be a leader globally in Environmental, Social and Governance.
environmental, social and governance leadership ambition.
Outlook
In 2020, the COVID-19 pandemic was undoubtedly the largest-scale public health emergency in our living memory. Affected businesses
and households received unprecedented public financial and financial support, which helped mitigate some of the extreme financial
impacts caused by the pandemic and accelerated the pace of recovery, which was particularly evident in countries with well-established

health policies and infrastructure. However, there were significant differences between countries, and under these challenging circumstances, Asia (excluding Japan) recorded the relatively strongest gross domestic product performance globally. Currently, the global economy is just beginning to emerge from a deep recession and entering the initial stages of improvement.
section
Although the impact of the pandemic has not yet ended, we believe 2021 will be a transitional year and we remain cautiously optimistic.
Market expectations for the rollout of vaccination programmes and new therapies are expected to help reduce infection rates and
severity of the disease, enabling healthcare systems to respond to the pandemic more effectively and reduce mortality rates. While we

do not anticipate that travel and consumer behaviour will return to pre-pandemic levels in the near term, as consumer confidence recovers, the recovery is expected to gradually broaden from manufacturing-led industries to consumption and service sectors. This will be particularly evident in countries with younger populations.
I am confident about AIA's long-term prospects. Despite near-term uncertainties, strong domestic demand drivers and key demographic
trends across Asia provide positive structural support for our long-term business outlook. With rising incomes, low private insurance
penetration, and limited social welfare coverage, demand for AIA's products will continue to grow. Leveraging our substantial
competitive advantages and strong growth track record, we will build a more sustainable future for our communities and create shared value for all stakeholders.
Lee Yuen Cheung
Group CEO and President
March 12, 2021

2020 Annual Report 015
Financial and Operating Review
Group Chief Financial Officer's Review
Despite unprecedented 2020 conditions, AIA achieved
Strong financial performance despite New Business Value impact from pandemic travel restrictions
We recorded Operating Profit After Tax and Underlying Free Surplus Generation
and solid growth in EV Equity. Our financial position remains very robust,
This enables us to continue investing amid numerous growth opportunities.
Growth rates and commentary on constant exchange rate basis.
Summary and Key Financial Highlights
In 2020, AIA recorded strong financial performance amid unprecedented pandemic-driven business and macroeconomic conditions.
Although mobility restrictions reduced VONB in 2020, recovery in sales momentum followed as business adjusted and restrictions
eased, continuing into 2021 with VONB growing 15% in the first two months versus 2020. Our prudent financial management and
consistent strategic focus on high-quality business have delivered continued OPAT and UFSG growth, robust capital position, and increased dividend per share
Further growth.
Mr. Garth Jones Group Chief Financial Officer
016 AIA Group Limited
The pandemic's principal impact on our 2020 financial performance was on new business sales, as containment measures across many
markets restricted face-to-face sales activities, particularly in H1 2020. Although VONB of US$2.765 billion in 2020 decreased 33%
versus 2019, we rapidly adopted digital tools, and widespread relaxation of movement restrictions supported sales momentum recovery. AIA China
contributed the largest share to the Group's VONB and was also our first reporting segment to demonstrate a recovery in sales momentum, as this region began to gradually
ease strict lockdowns from March 2020 onwards. Excluding sales from Mainland Chinese visitors, which remained at low levels, the Group's other segments showed strong
recovery in VONB in the second half, growing 23% compared to the first half. The Group's new business momentum remained robust into 2021, with VONB in the first two
months 15% higher than the corresponding period in 2020.
Embedded value equity as at 31 December 2020 reached a new high of US$67.185 billion, an increase of US$5.277 billion before dividend payments of US$1.997 billion,
This was due to EV operating profit of US$7.243 billion offsetting lower government bond yields. EV operating profit included positive
operating variances of US$549 million from favorable experience versus EV assumptions.
Our high-quality, recurring earnings sources and active management of our in-force portfolio drove OPAT growth of 5% to US$5.942 billion, while
maintaining an operating margin of 16.9%. Renewal premiums collected increased by 10%, with recurring premiums representing over 90% of total
premiums collected in 2020. OPAT growth benefited from significant positive claims experience throughout the year, which offset the decline in yields on
new fixed income investments and the impact of reduced assumed long-term equity investment returns. As expected, the exceptional positive medical
claims experience noted in the first half did not recur in the second half, but we continued to see positive contributions to OPAT growth from this source.
With AIA's Thailand business lapse experience normalizing, persistency improved in the second half, with the Group's persistency at 95%.
UFSG of US$5.843 billion grew 7%, driven by continued growth of our in-force portfolio and active management thereof, offsetting lower
expected investment returns. As at 31 December 2020, the Group's financial position remained strong, with Free Surplus of US$13.473
billion after dividend payments of US$1.997 billion.
As at 31 December 2020, our principal operating company AIA Co.'s solvency ratio remained very strong at 489%. As at 31 December
2020, under the new Group regulatory framework, the Group LCSM cover ratio was 374%. Going forward, we will disclose the Group
LCSM cover ratio as the primary measure of the Group's regulatory solvency position.
The Board recommends an increase in the final dividend of 7.5% to 100.30 Hong Kong cents per share, subject to approval by
shareholders at the Company's forthcoming Annual General Meeting. This brings the total dividend for 2020 to 135.30 Hong Kong cents

per share, representing an increase of 6.9% compared to the total dividend for 2019. This reflects AIA's long-established prudent, sustainable and progressive dividend policy, which enables the Group to capitalize on future growth opportunities while maintaining financial flexibility.
We remain confident in AIA's business opportunities across Asia. We will continue deploying capital to achieve profitable new business
growth, leveraging competitive advantages while maintaining prudent financial management and delivering long-term shareholder value.

Although VONB of US$2,765 million in 2020 decreased 33% from 2019, swift adoption of digital tools and widespread relaxation of
travel restrictions supported sales momentum recovery. AIA China contributed most to Group VONB in 2020 and was the first segment
demonstrating recovery, as the region progressively relaxed lockdowns from March 2020. Excluding subdued mainland Chinese visitor
sales to Hong Kong, other segments' VONB rebounded strongly in H2, growing 23% versus H1. Group new business momentum
remained robust in 2021, with VONB in the first two months up 15% versus the same period in 2020.
AIA China achieved strong momentum in 2021, with VONB in the first two months recording very robust growth versus 2020. VONB of
US$968 million in 2020 decreased 17% from 2019, primarily due to limited sales during the early COVID-19 outbreak in Q1. As travel
restrictions eased, new business momentum improved rapidly. VONB resumed customary seasonal patterns with greater concentration
in H1. In 2020, AIA China contributed the largest share to Group VONB.
Following travel restrictions, new business sales from Mainland Chinese visitors to AIA Hong Kong were effectively suspended from
early February 2020, causing significant VONB decline. VONB margin decreased due to reduced new business volume, underwriting
expense overruns, product mix changes, and lower interest rates. Macau resumed the Individual Visit Scheme for Mainland Chinese
visitors in late September 2020, supporting new business sales recovery from Mainland visitors, which exceeded one-third of AIA Macau's annualized new premiums in Q4.
018 AIA Group Limited
